blocks.content: Eighth grade started the way things started when you already knew how they worked. No first-day nerves — just the same building, one year heavier.
Kit had spent most of the summer talking to Brooke on AIM — nothing serious, nothing named. By the time school started, she sat at their lunch table without it being a question.

blocks.speaker: Eli
blocks.content: Presidential campaign. Year two. I need a slogan.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: You already won. Why do you need a slogan?

blocks.speaker: Eli
blocks.content: Incumbents need slogans more than challengers. It's about maintaining momentum.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: How about: still here, still Eli.

blocks.speaker: Eli
blocks.content: I love that. I'm using that.
Kit watched Daisy laugh at that and looked back at his lunch.
